channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Freedom is bittersweet for Palestinians released from Israeli jails
item.description: Dozens of Palestinian women and teenagers were released from Israeli prisons this week as part of a deal between Israel and Hamas to pause the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After 80 years, not many Auschwitz survivors are left. One man makes telling the stories his mission
item.description: HAIFA, Israel (AP) — Naftali Fürst will never forget his first view of the Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp, on Nov. 3, 1944. He was 12 years old. SS soldiers threw open the doors of the cattle car, where he was crammed in with his mother, father, brother, and more than 80 others.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Asylum-seekers pushed to new extremes in Mexico after Trump's border crackdown begins
item.description: Migrants in Mexico who were hoping to come to the U.S. are adjusting to a new and uncertain reality after President Donald Trump began cracking down on border security.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky And Edgar H. Clemente, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trudeau says Americans will pay more whenever Trump decides to impose tariffs on Canada
item.description: Outgoing Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says prices for Americans will go up if President Donald Trump follows through with his vow to apply sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy's top court upholds Amanda Knox's conviction for falsely accusing innocent man of murder
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s highest court on Thursday confirmed a slander conviction against U.S. defendant Amanda Knox for accusing an innocent man of murdering her British flatmate 17 years ago in a sensational case that polarized trial watchers on both sides of the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urges Putin to ‘make a deal’ to end the war in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump on Thursday urged Russian President Vladimir Putin to make a deal to end the war in Ukraine, arguing he thinks the latter is ready to negotiate.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: A 2,000-year-old statue is found abandoned in a garbage bag in Greece
item.description: A marble statue believed to be more than 2,000 years old has been found in a garbage bag near the city of Thessaloniki in Greece; experts say the piece dates back to the Hellenistic era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump holds first foreign leader call with Saudi crown prince
item.description: President Trump held his first official call with a foreign leader since being sworn in for his second term with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, a controversial figure who Trump on Thursday praised as a "fantastic guy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: House pair introduces bill to end China's preferred trade status
item.description: Legislation to revoke China’s Permanent Normal Trade Relations was introduced Thursday by a bipartisan pair of House lawmakers, building on a Republican effort last year to repeal Beijing’s preferential trade status. The bill would solidify efforts by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin sees nothing 'new' in Trump's tariff threats over Ukraine war
item.description: The Kremlin is unmoved by President Trump’s threats to impose sanctions if Russia does not bow out of its conflict with Ukraine. “We don't see any particularly new elements here.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: At least 12 train passengers killed in India after jumping onto tracks to flee alleged fire
item.description: In India, at least 12 train passengers died and at least six others were injured when they jumped out of their train cars to escape a rumored fire and were struck by another train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine is reforming its recruitment efforts to attract younger soldiers and boost forces
item.description: Ukraine is in the final stages of drafting recruitment reforms to attract 18- to 25-year-olds who are currently exempt from mobilization as it looks for ways to bolster its fighting force.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Putin reportedly concerned over Russia's economy ahead of possible Trump tariffs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is reportedly worried about the state of the country's economy amid threat of tariffs from the U.S. as President Donald Trump returns to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests warrants for Afghan Taliban leaders over persecution of women
item.description: The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor says he has requested arrest warrants for two top Afghan Taliban officials over the repression of women.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Saudi crown prince says kingdom intends to invest $600 billion in US during call with Trump
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crown prince says the kingdom wants to invest $600 billion in the United States over the next four years, comments that came after President Donald Trump mused about returning to the kingdom as his first foreign trip.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump redesignates Houthis as foreign terrorist organization
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday directed his administration to relist the Houthis in Yemen as a foreign terrorist organization (FTO) in an effort to crack down on the Iranian-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Panama protests 'threat' of force as Trump pushed to reclaim canal
item.description: The Panamanian government formally raised concerns with the United Nations over President Trump’s threats to retake the Panama Canal, noting any threat of force would violate rules. In a letter to U.N. Secretary General António Guterres, Panama points to U.N.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan Air Force officer killed after being ‘inhaled’ by fighter jet’s engine
item.description: A Taiwan Air Force officer died after being sucked into the engine of an Indigenous Defense Fighter jet at Ching Chuan Kang Air Base, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The State Department has suspended flights bringing previously approved refugees to the United States, cutting off access to protection in advance of the timeline set by President Trump in a new order pausing the program. An email reviewed Wednesday by The Associated Press, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Task Force created in Africa to counter terror fears from Iran and jihadi groups
item.description: South Africa’s Chief Rabbi Dr. Warren Goldstein has created an Anti-Terror Task Force to combat terror from Iran and Jihadi groups globally. The group will also try to protect Christians under attack in Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks out for first time, says she has 'returned to my life' following Hamas captivity
item.description: Emily Damari, one of the first hostages freed by Hamas in the cease-fire agreement, is speaking out for the first time following her release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bittersweet rejoicing as first hostages return to Israel after 471 days in captivity
item.description: Three hostages return to Israel after 471 days in Hamas captivity under a cease-fire deal. Israelis celebrate their return but face concerns about the release of convicted Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
